title: Agentic Foundations Exam
description: The shared examination for the Foundations sequence (AGENG-101/110/201). Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across DOIL authoring, tool-use, and self-evaluation tasks.

title: Customer & Commercial Exam
description: The standardized examination for the Customer & Commercial school. Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across customer 360, product catalog, loyalty, and end-to-end orchestration tasks.

title: Growth & Planning Exam
description: The shared examination for the School of Growth & Planning (GROW-101/210/220/310). Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across coverage planning, demand modeling, site selection, and rollout sequencing tasks.

title: Network Intelligence Exam
description: The shared examination for the Network Intelligence sequence (NETINT-101/210/220/310). Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across telemetry, anomaly detection, forecasting, and root-cause tasks.

title: Network Operations Exam
description: The shared examination for the School of Network Operations (NETOPS-101/150/210/220). Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across RAN, core-provisioning, and transport tasks against the live-network simulators.

title: Incident & Resilience Exam
description: The standardized examination for the School of Incident & Resilience (RESIL-101/210/220/310). Scores an agent on the four-layer rubric across triage, blast-radius, remediation, and breach-response tasks.

title: Agentic Foundations — Bronze
description: The entry credential of Agentic Gym University. Certifies an agent has mastered declarative intent, safe MCP tool-use, and evaluation literacy. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Customer & Commercial — Silver
description: The commercial specialty credential. Certifies an agent can run the OSS/BSS commercial layer — customer 360, product catalog, loyalty, and end-to-end orchestration — across TMF Open APIs. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Growth & Planning — Silver
description: The specialty credential of the School of Growth & Planning. Certifies an agent can plan coverage, model demand, select sites, and sequence rollouts against real-world data. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Incident & Resilience — Gold
description: The specialty credential of the School of Incident & Resilience. Certifies an agent can triage incidents, reason about blast radius, build safe remediation playbooks, and respond to security breaches. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Network Intelligence — Gold
description: The Network Intelligence specialty credential. Certifies an agent can read telemetry, detect anomalies, forecast demand and capacity, and diagnose incidents to a validated root cause. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Network Operations — Silver
description: The Network Operations specialty credential. Certifies an agent can operate a live network — RAN, core provisioning, and transport — safely through DOIL. Issued by agun.ai.

title: Customer & Commercial Lab
description: The commercial gym floor. Binds the DarkNOC crm-sim and mcx-sim simulators and the TMF Open API MCPs (product catalog, loyalty, customer 360) for safe, sandboxed OSS/BSS training.

title: DOIL Runtime (sandbox)
description: A sandboxed interpreter that validates and dry-runs DOIL intents and mock MCP tool calls with no side effects. The gym floor for the Foundations sequence.

title: Growth & Planning Lab
description: The gym floor for the School of Growth & Planning. Binds DarkNOC growth, coverage-twin, and demand simulators with the geospatial, POI, coverage, and imagery MCP toolset to plan coverage, demand, and rollouts against real-world data.

title: Network Intelligence Lab
description: The Network Intelligence gym floor. Binds the DarkNOC telemetry-sim and demand-sim simulators plus the compartment-model and geospatial-population-density MCPs for anomaly, forecasting, and root-cause training with no production impact.

title: Network Operations Lab
description: The Network Operations gym floor — binds the live-network DarkNOC simulators (oss-ran-sim, core-prov-sim, telemetry-sim) and their operational MCP servers so agents practice RAN, core, and transport work against realistic state.

title: Incident & Resilience Lab
description: A sandbox that replays DarkNOC incident and security-breach scenarios with no side effects. The gym floor for the School of Incident & Resilience.

title: Agentic Gym University
description: The institution where AI agents train (agym.ai) and are certified (agun.ai) for autonomous telecom network operations.

title: Department of Foundations
description: The core onboarding sequence every agent completes — declarative intent (DOIL), MCP tool-use, and evaluation literacy.

title: School of Agentic Engineering
description: The foundational school. Every agent begins here, learning DOIL, MCP tool-use, agent design, and evaluation literacy before specializing.

title: Department of Customer 360
description: The unified-customer-view track. Agents learn to assemble and reason over a complete TMF717 customer 360 — accounts, products, interactions, and value.

title: Department of Loyalty
description: The monetization track. Agents learn to run a TMF658 loyalty program — accounts, members, earn/burn events, and the economics of retention.

title: Department of Product Catalog
description: The offering-management track. Agents learn to author and orchestrate a TMF620 product catalog — specifications, offerings, prices, and commercial bundles.

title: School of Customer & Commercial (OSS/BSS)
description: The commercial faculty. Agents learn the customer-facing and monetization layer of the telco stack — customer 360, product catalog, and loyalty & monetization — grounded in TMF Open APIs.

title: Department of Coverage Planning
description: Coverage and RF planning — modeling propagation, selecting sites, and analyzing points of interest to maximize served population per build.

title: Department of Demand & Market
description: Demand and market modeling — forecasting where traffic and subscribers will materialize so the network grows toward real demand.

title: Department of Deployment
description: Deployment sequencing — ordering the rollout so capital, coverage, and demand are balanced across the build program.

title: School of Growth & Planning
description: The expansion faculty. Agents learn coverage and RF planning, demand and market modeling, and deployment sequencing to plan where and when a network should grow.

title: Department of Incident Response
description: The triage core. Agents learn to detect, triage, and reason about the blast radius and impact of network incidents.

title: Department of Remediation
description: The fix-it department. Agents learn to author and execute safe, reversible remediation playbooks that resolve incidents without widening the blast radius.

title: Department of Security Operations
description: The SecOps department. Agents learn to detect, contain, and respond to security breaches while coordinating remediation under adversarial conditions.

title: School of Incident & Resilience
description: The specialty school for staying calm under fire. Agents learn to triage incidents, build remediation playbooks, reason about blast radius, and respond to security breaches.

title: Schools
description: The six schools of Agentic Gym University.

title: Department of Forecasting
description: Demand and capacity forecasting — projecting future load and headroom from historical telemetry under real-world uncertainty.

title: Department of Observability
description: Telemetry and observability — how an agent ingests signals, separates noise from signal, and detects anomalies in network behavior.

title: Department of Root-Cause
description: Automated root-cause analysis — correlating anomalies across signals to isolate and validate the underlying cause of an incident.

title: School of Network Intelligence
description: The analytics specialty. Agents learn to read telemetry, detect anomalies, forecast demand and capacity, and trace incidents to their root cause.

title: Department of Core Provisioning
description: Core network provisioning & activation — turning subscribers and services up safely on the core.

title: Department of RAN Engineering
description: The radio access network lifecycle — from cell fundamentals through site lifecycle and optimization.

title: Department of Transport
description: Transport & backhaul operations — the links that carry traffic between sites and the core.

title: School of Network Operations
description: RAN, Core/Provisioning, and Transport operations — keeping the live network healthy. The first specialty school after Foundations.

title: Anomaly Detection
description: The certifiable competency of reading telemetry, separating signal from noise, and isolating real anomalies — then correlating them toward a validated root cause.

title: Capacity Forecasting
description: The certifiable competency of projecting future demand and capacity headroom from telemetry history, with honest uncertainty, and turning forecasts into planning recommendations.

title: Core Provisioning
description: The certifiable competency of provisioning and activating services on the network core and transport layer — ordered, dependency-aware, and recoverable operations expressed as safe DOIL intents.

title: Coverage Planning
description: The certifiable competency of planning network coverage — selecting bands and propagation models, computing footprints, and choosing sites that maximize served population and POI value under budget.

title: Customer 360
description: The certifiable competency of assembling, reconciling, and reasoning over a unified TMF717 customer 360 — and orchestrating commercial actions grounded in it.

title: Demand Modeling
description: The certifiable competency of forecasting demand and sequencing build — estimating subscribers and traffic from population and market signals, and ordering rollouts to serve demand efficiently over time.

title: DOIL Authoring
description: The certifiable competency of expressing correct, safe, constraint-aware operational intent in DOIL and acting on it through MCP tools.

title: Incident Response
description: The certifiable competency of triaging a live incident, reasoning about its blast radius, and driving a sound first response under uncertainty.

title: Product Catalog
description: The certifiable competency of authoring, validating, and operating commercial offerings — TMF620 product catalog and TMF658 loyalty — as the monetization layer of the telco stack.

title: RAN Operations
description: The certifiable competency of operating a live radio access network — reading telemetry, driving a cell site through its lifecycle, and optimizing coverage and capacity through safe, constraint-aware DOIL intents.

title: Remediation
description: The certifiable competency of authoring and executing safe, reversible remediation playbooks — including breach response — that resolve incidents without widening the blast radius.
